ai_summary: India Power Corporation Limited (IPCL) is currently undergoing a Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) as per an NCLT order from May 15, 2026, signifying severe financial distress and the transfer of operational control to an Interim Resolution Professional. The company has scheduled its second Committee of Creditors (CoC) meeting for July 20, 2026, which is a critical event for determining its future, including potential restructuring or liquidation. This situation represents a major red flag for investors, as existing equity is highly likely to face significant impairment or a complete wipeout.
